methods.description: Process for collecting and preparing sediment samples for pollen and total organic carbon (TOC) analysis.

steps.description: The upper~27 cm of a sediment monolith were excavated, photographed, and stored in a cold room (4 • C) at the Institute of Geology, China Earthquake Administration (IGCEA) in 2016.
The sediments were sub-sampled at 1-cm interval for pollen and total organic carbon (TOC) analysis.

methods.description: Procedure for processing sediment and moss samples for pollen analysis.

steps.description: Approximately 5-18 g of each subsample was processed following palynological procedures, including treatments with 15% HCl and 3% NaOH, heavy liquid flotation with KI solution, treatments with 40% HF and 15% HCl, and sieving (7 µm) if necessary.
steps.searchable_summary: process subsamples with chemical treatments and sieving

steps.description: For the moss samples, no acetolysis treatment was done because the pollen grains were clean and contained no protoplasm when viewed under a microscope.
One tablet of Lycopodium spores was added to each sample in order to estimate the pollen concentration.
The prepared specimens were mounted in glycerol for pollen identification.

methods.description: Counting and analysis of pollen grains for percentage calculation and diagram plotting.

steps.description: For moss samples, approximately 450 pollen grains (excluding spores) per sample were counted and were used as the pollen sum for pollen percentage calculation.
steps.searchable_summary: count pollen grains for percentage calculation

steps.description: Pollen diagrams were plotted using Grapher software, and all samples were grouped by cluster analysis (CONISS).
steps.searchable_summary: plot pollen diagrams and perform cluster analysis

methods.description: Measurement of TOC contents in sediment samples using an Elementar Rapid CS analyzer.

steps.description: TOC contents were measured with an Elementar Rapid CS analyzer following procedures described in (Xiao et al 2006).
steps.searchable_summary: measure TOC contents using Elementar Rapid CS analyzer

methods.description: Construction of an age model using 210 Pb and 137 Cs dating methods.

steps.description: The age model is based on a combination of 210 Pb and 137 Cs dating methods.
steps.searchable_summary: construct age model using 210 Pb and 137 Cs dating

steps.description: The bulk-weighed, dry samples were sealed in plastic test tubes with caps for 210 Pb dating by gamma spectrometry with well-type coaxial low background intrinsic germanium detectors (Ortec HP Ge GWL series) at the Nanjing Institute of Geography and Limnology, Chinese Academy of Sciences.
steps.searchable_summary: perform 210 Pb dating using gamma spectrometry

steps.description: The 210 Pb activity was determined via gamma emissions at 46.5 keV.
steps.searchable_summary: determine 210 Pb activity via gamma emissions

steps.description: The emissions of 226 Ra with the 295 keV and 352 keV γ-rays emitted by its daughter nuclide 214 Pb were determined after 3 weeks of storage in sealed containers to allow for radioactive equilibrium.
steps.searchable_summary: determine emissions of 226 Ra and 214 Pb

steps.description: The radioactivity of 137 Cs was measured with the 662 keV photo peak.
